questions.assertion: A queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure.
questions.reason: A queue follows the FIFO principle, where the first element that is added to the queue will be the first one to be removed.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The assertion states that a queue is a FIFO data structure, and the reason explains why, making option (A) the correct choice.

questions.assertion: A queue is a Linear Data Structure.
questions.reason: A queue is a collection of elements in which each element is a node, and nodes are arranged in a sequence, making it a linear data structure.

questions.assertion: Dequeue operation in a queue is an O(1) operation.
questions.reason: Dequeue operation in a queue requires removing the front element, which is a constant time operation, making it an O(1) operation.

questions.assertion: Enqueue operation in a queue is an O(n) operation.
